column2: You are selecting replaceable memory for a typical laptop. Which module form factor should you look for?
column4:
Why

SO-DIMM is the compact memory-module form factor commonly used in laptops and other space-constrained systems.

Exam Trap

Do not assume every laptop has a replaceable module; some systems use memory soldered to the system board.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.1; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1
column9: A+::220-1201::1.1 A+::220-1201::LaptopHardware Basic HighYield Memory Upgrade

column2: A laptop has failed RAM that is soldered to the system board. What hardware must typically be replaced to correct the memory hardware failure?
column4:
Why

Soldered RAM is not a removable module, so there is no separate SO-DIMM to exchange.

Real Help Desk

Confirm whether memory is modular or soldered before promising an upgrade or quoting a repair.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.1; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1

column2: A laptop owner prioritizes quiet operation and lower storage access latency. Should the replacement use an HDD or an SSD?
column4:
Why

An SSD stores data without spinning platters or other moving parts, supporting quiet operation and faster access.

Exam Trap

“Better” depends on the stated requirement. This card selects an SSD because latency and noise are explicit priorities.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.1; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1
column9: A+::220-1201::1.1 A+::220-1201::LaptopHardware Basic HighYield Storage Comparison

column2: Why should a technician verify the exact laptop model and service procedure before estimating a battery replacement?
column3: Battery access is model-specific: it may be modular or may require substantial laptop disassembly.
column4:
Why

Battery chemistry does not determine how the battery is mounted or accessed. The physical design controls the repair process.

Real Help Desk

Avoid turning an illustrative repair time or screw count into a universal procedure.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.1; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1

column2: A technician is preparing to replace a laptop keyboard that uses screws and a ribbon cable. What should determine the correct disassembly and reconnection sequence?
column3: The manufacturer's service documentation for that laptop model.
column4:
Why

Keyboard access varies by model. Service documentation identifies the required fasteners, access order, and ribbon-cable handling for the specific design.

Exam Trap

The presence of screws and a ribbon cable does not make the complete replacement procedure universal across laptops.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.1; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1

column2: What should a technician consult before attempting to remove or replace an individual laptop keycap?
column4:
Why

Keycaps and the mechanisms beneath them are delicate, and removal methods differ between designs.

Exam Trap

Do not treat every laptop keycap as if it uses the same release method.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.1; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1
column9: A+::220-1201::1.1 A+::220-1201::LaptopHardware Basic Keyboard Keycap RepairStrategy

column2: During an HDD-to-SSD migration, the user wants to preserve the existing operating system, applications, settings, and documents. Which migration approach best meets that requirement?
column3: Image or clone the HDD to the SSD with appropriate imaging software.
column4:
Why

Cloning or restoring an image carries the existing software environment forward, avoiding a complete operating-system and application reinstall.

Exam Trap

A clean installation is a different valid strategy, but it does not satisfy the requirement to preserve the existing installation directly.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.1; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1
column9: A+::220-1201::1.1 A+::220-1201::LaptopHardware Basic HighYield Storage Migration

column2: What is the practical difference between creating a drive image file and performing a direct drive-to-drive clone?
column3: An image creates an intermediate file; a clone copies directly between drives.
column4:
Why

Both approaches can migrate an existing installation, but they differ in whether an intermediate image file is created.

Real Help Desk

Either workflow requires suitable imaging software and a way to connect the needed storage devices.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.1; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1
column9: A+::220-1201::1.1 A+::220-1201::LaptopHardware Basic Storage Imaging Comparison

column2: A workstation should authenticate a user when a supported device is tapped within a few centimeters, without requiring a typed password. Which laptop feature fits this scenario?
column4:
Why

NFC is designed for very short-range exchanges and can support authentication interactions.

Exam Trap

Bluetooth also connects nearby devices, but its personal-area range and pairing model do not match this tap-at-the-reader scenario.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.2; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1

column2: A laptop must support sign-in using “something you are.” Which physical security feature meets that requirement?
column3: Biometrics, such as a fingerprint reader or face-recognition hardware.
column4:
Why

Fingerprint and facial characteristics are biometric factors. The feature also requires appropriate operating-system configuration.

Exam Trap

Having a camera or sensor does not by itself prove that biometric sign-in is configured.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.2; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1

column2: A user needs to record audio beyond casual video calls, and the built-in laptop microphone does not meet the requirement. What input option should the technician recommend?
column4:
Why

The built-in display microphone is convenient for everyday calls. The private source recommends an external microphone for non-casual use.

Source Note

This card makes no universal audio-quality claim; it follows the source's distinction between casual built-in use and an external option for other recording needs.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.2; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1

column2: A technician opens a laptop display assembly and sees multiple thin wires routed around the screen. Which wireless hardware are these most likely associated with?
column4:
Why

Laptop Wi-Fi antenna wires are commonly routed around the screen, where the antennas can sit high in the system.

Source Note

This card tests sourced antenna placement only; it does not infer a fault or prescribe a repair procedure.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 pp.1-2; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.1
column9: A+::220-1201::1.1 A+::220-1201::LaptopHardware Basic HighYield WiFi Antenna Identification
